chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يُجْزِئُ مِنْ غُسْلِ الْإِحْرَامِ وَ مَا لَا يُجْزِئُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 78 – What suffices from washing for the Ihraam and what does not suffice

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/252)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/252)
text: إِذَا اغْتَسَلَ الرَّجُلُ وَ هُوَ يُرِيدُ أَنْ يُحْرِمَ فَلَبِسَ قَمِيصاً قَبْلَ أَنْ يُلَبِّيَ فَعَلَيْهِ الْغُسْلُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ala’a Bin Razeyn, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws having said: ‘When the man washes and he intends to wear Ihraam, so he wears a shirt (instead) before he exclaims Talbiyya, then upon him is (repeating of) the washing’.
en.sarwar: 8. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad, from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-‘Ala’ ibn Razin from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “This is about a man who takes Ghusl (bath) for Ihram, then wears a shirt before saying Talbiyah. Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘He is required to take Ghusl (bath) again.’”
